Pat Daley

⚓   Protecting Our Coast  ·  Supporting Our Families  ·  Putting the Islands First   ⚓

Pat Daley — Wilmington Island resident & candidate for HD 166

⛳ Golfer (mixed results)
🎣 Coastal fisherman
🐾 Frank’s human
Meet Pat

A Neighbor, Not a Politician.

Pat Daley spent four years in downtown Savannah before putting down roots on Wilmington Island six years ago. He’s not parachuting in — he pays the same bills, worries about the same things, and loves this coast as much as anyone here.

He’s an engineer by training (UGA, 2015) and a business leader by experience (Georgia Southern MBA, 2024). He started at the bottom in Savannah’s manufacturing industry and worked his way up through hard work and accountability.

Why He’s Running

Pat got tired of watching the cost of living squeeze the people he calls neighbors. Nobody should have to leave the Islands because they can’t afford to stay.

He believes District 166 deserves someone who listens before they talk, tells the truth even when it’s hard, and doesn’t answer to anyone but the people who live here.

Platform

What Pat Stands For

No fluff. No platitudes. Just the things that actually matter to the people of District 166.

🏠

Affordability & Cost of Living

+

Pat pays the same bills you do. Property taxes, insurance, groceries — it’s a lot. He won’t let Atlanta politicians make it worse.

  • Fight unfunded state mandates that drive up local costs
  • Tax relief for seniors and retirees on fixed incomes
  • Responsible budgeting that protects services without raising taxes
  • Full transparency on how your tax dollars are spent
🌊

Protecting Our Coast & Environment

+

Our marshes and barrier islands aren’t just beautiful — they’re our economy, our identity, and our kids’ inheritance. Pat will never stop fighting for them.

  • Strong coastal and marsh protections — no compromise
  • Flood mitigation, drainage, and storm resilience funding
  • Smart growth that respects what makes the Islands special
  • Local decisions made locally — not dictated from Atlanta
🏪

Supporting Local Small Businesses

+

The family-owned shops and local services that make District 166 special deserve a rep who has their back — not more red tape.

  • Cut unnecessary regulations so local businesses can grow
  • Workforce development that connects local workers with local jobs
  • State resources to help small businesses stay competitive
🏥

Affordable Healthcare Access

+

Healthcare shouldn’t be a luxury — especially on the Islands where a doctor visit can mean a long drive. Pat is focused on practical solutions, not politics.

  • Responsible Medicaid expansion for working families and seniors
  • Protect local clinics, hospitals, and emergency services
  • Expand telehealth so island residents get care without the commute
  • Lower costs through price transparency and cutting waste
⚖️

Independent, Ethical Leadership

+

Pat doesn’t owe favors. He answers to one group of people: you.

  • Shows up — every vote, every meeting, every call
  • Listens before deciding — your voice comes first
  • Tells the truth, even when it’s uncomfortable
  • Delivers results for District 166, not for donors
Why Pat

The Daley Difference

District 166 doesn’t need another politician who shows up every four years. It needs someone who already lives here, already gets it, and isn’t for sale.

🏝️

Rooted Here

Six years on Wilmington Island, with four before that in downtown Savannah. Pat isn’t learning about District 166 on the campaign trail — he already knows it.

🔬

Practical Experience

Engineering degree. MBA. Worked his way from entry-level to management in Savannah industry. He knows how to solve problems, not just talk about them.

🌿

Coast First

Our marshes and barrier islands define this community. Pat will protect them from overdevelopment and push back on decisions made without local input.

🤝

Independent

He keeps his commitments small and his accountability large. He answers to the people of District 166 — that’s it.

Join the Campaign

Get Involved

Local races are won by neighbors talking to neighbors. If you believe District 166 deserves better, show up — even for an hour.

Volunteer with Pat

Knock doors. Make calls. Plant yard signs. Help at events. Whatever you can give — an hour or a month — it matters. Sign up and we’ll reach out.

Send Pat a Message

Got a question? A concern? Something on your mind about the Islands? Pat reads these. His team responds.

Show Your Support

Wear the Movement

Every shirt, hat, and bumper on the road is a conversation starter. Help us put the Islands First — one neighbor at a time.
